summary: Validated Docker backend build fix: Dockerfile filter for duplicated litellm line + pip resolution + login regression. All 3 checks passed.

checks_performed.name: Dockerfile contains grep -v '^litellm @' filter + --extra-index-url
checks_performed.evidence: Line 16-17 of /app/backend/Dockerfile: RUN grep -v "^litellm @" requirements.txt > /tmp/req.txt && pip install -r /tmp/req.txt --extra-index-url https://d33sy5i8bnduwe.cloudfront.net/simple/

checks_performed.name: pip dry-run resolution reproduces Docker RUN step
checks_performed.evidence: Exit code 0. No ResolutionImpossible. Filtered req has 134 lines (litellm line removed). Resolved plan includes litellm-1.80.0 (pulled transitively via emergentintegrations==0.2.0) and emergentintegrations-0.2.0.

checks_performed.name: Regression: POST /api/auth/login demo@logirent.fr/demo1234
checks_performed.evidence: HTTP 200, JWT token returned, user id 1e2e37fe-9e48-4c6d-aeaa-17368a83594b, email demo@logirent.fr

critical_code_review_comments: Dockerfile fix is correct and minimal. Comment (line 14-15) properly documents intent.

Consider a cleaner long-term fix: remove the direct 'litellm @ ...' line from requirements.txt entirely since emergentintegrations already pins the same URL. This would eliminate the need for the grep workaround in Dockerfile.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Docker build fix validated via pip dry-run (Docker not available in preview env). If real Docker build is possible in deployment env, retest with actual `docker build` on the VPS.
rca of the issue: requirements.txt had both 'emergentintegrations==0.2.0' (line 24) and 'litellm @ https://...whl' (line 61). Since emergentintegrations declares the same litellm URL as a direct dependency, pip 24+ treats duplicate direct-URL references as conflicts (ResolutionImpossible). Dockerfile fix strips the redundant top-level litellm line before pip install, letting pip resolve litellm transitively via emergentintegrations. Validated with pip --dry-run: resolves cleanly, both packages present.
